questions.question: Which failure can occur if an AES-GCM nonce is reused with the same key?
options: Confidentiality and integrity can be compromised
questions.description: GCM nonce reuse is dangerous and can expose plaintext relationships and authentication weaknesses.

questions.question: Which password hashing property slows offline cracking?
questions.description: Algorithms such as Argon2, bcrypt, and scrypt use tunable cost to slow attackers.

questions.question: Which attack targets weak randomness during key generation?
questions.description: Poor entropy can make generated keys guessable or reproducible.
